content.data:
# AGENTS.md - Prosper Quick Reference

> **Full conventions & commands** → `.kiro/steering.md`  
> **Project patterns** → `.kiro/skills/prosper-patterns/SKILL.md`  
> **Feature docs** → `docs/features/`

---

## Known Gotchas

| Gotcha | Fix |
|--------|-----|
| PHP & Node.js not on host | Use `docker exec prosper_app_dev <cmd>` or `make npm cmd="..."` |
| AutoCompleteInput refs not populated | Call `setQuery()` after `nextTick` |
| PHPStan cascades from base class | Fix `BaseCrud`/`BaseCrudDto` errors first |
| Missing property types on child controllers | Child controllers MUST redeclare typed properties matching base class |
| Decimal part stripped as Number | Never convert decimal part to `Number` in CurrencyInput2 |
| Litepicker range not displayed | Check for ` - ` separator before parsing as single date |
| `team` stored as name string, not ID | `closing.team` and `dncn.team` are team `name`, not `team.id` |
| `approval_status` is int-backed enum | Cast to `DncnApprovalStatusEnum` in model `$casts` |
| `pointer-events-none` hides file links | Use `:disabled` on individual inputs instead |
| `unset_array` strips unexpected fields | Check `__prepareDataStore`'s `unset_array` if a field isn't saving |
| TomSelect hidden in overflow-hidden | Change filter card to `overflow-visible`, add `!z-[60]` |

---

## CRUD Checklist

When adding a new CRUD resource, follow in order:

### 1. Migration
`database/migrations/{Module}/` — Use `$table->id()` + `$table->uuid()`. Include `HasBaseOwner` columns.

### 2. Model
`app/Models/{Module}/` — Traits: `HasBaseOwner`, `HasBaseTable`, `LogsActivity`. Include all 6 base owner fields in `$fillable`.

### 3. DTOs
- **Resource**: `app/DTOs/Resources/{Name}Dto.php` — extends `Data`, implements `DataPreparableInterface`, uses `HasDtoPrepareTrait`
- **Request**: `app/DTOs/Requests/Create{Name}Dto.php` — extends `Data`, defines `static rules(): array`

### 4. Controller
- **Web**: `app/Http/Controllers/Web/{Module}/{Name}Controller.php` extends `BaseWebCrudDto`
- **API**: `app/Http/Controllers/Api/{Module}/{Name}Controller.php` extends `BaseCrudDto`

```php
class ExampleController extends BaseWebCrudDto
{
    public string $model = Example::class;
    public string $viewPath = 'Module/Example';
    public string $viewEdit = '/Create';
    public string $resourceDto = ExampleDto::class;
    public string $storeDto = CreateExampleDto::class;
    public string $updateDto = CreateExampleDto::class;
    public string $modelKey = 'uuid';
    public bool $use_redirect = false;
    /** @var array<string> */
    public array $searchAble = ['name', 'code'];
    public bool $autoCache = true;
}
```

### 5. Policy
`app/Policies/{Module}/{Name}Policy.php` — Register in `AuthServiceProvider`.

### 6. Routes
- Web: `routes/Web/{Module}/route.php`
- API: `routes/Api/{Module}/route.php`

### 7. Frontend API
```ts
// resources/js/apis/{name}.api.ts
export const exampleApi = {
    index: (params?: object) => route('module.examples.index', params),
    store: () => route('module.examples.store'),
    update: (params: { uuid: string }) => route('module.examples.update', params),
    destroy: (params: { uuid: string }) => route('module.examples.destroy', params),
}
```

### 8. Frontend Page
- `resources/js/Pages/{Module}/{Name}/Index.vue`
- `resources/js/Pages/{Module}/{Name}/Create.vue`
- TypeScript interface in `resources/js/types/{module}.ts`
- i18n entries in `resources/js/i18n/{module}/{locale}.ts`

---

## Feature Documentation Index

| Feature | Doc |
|---------|-----|
| Closing approval & save flow | [docs/features/closing-workflow.md](docs/features/closing-workflow.md) |
| DNCN multi-level approval | [docs/features/dncn-workflow.md](docs/features/dncn-workflow.md) |
| Endorsement form | [docs/features/endorsement.md](docs/features/endorsement.md) |
| Insured (banker clause, journal type, seeders) | [docs/features/insured.md](docs/features/insured.md) |
| Team ID migration | [docs/features/team-id-migration.md](docs/features/team-id-migration.md) |
| Component fixes (CurrencyInput2, Litepicker) | [docs/features/component-fixes.md](docs/features/component-fixes.md) |

---

## Rules

- **NEVER commit or push without explicit user permission.**
- All commands run via Docker — no PHP/Node on host.
- Fix base class errors first — they cascade to all child controllers.

# Prosper
Premiro Revenue Operations & Finance System

## Local development

1. Install [Docker](https://docs.docker.com/engine/install/) and [Docker Compose](https://docs.docker.com/compose/install/) on your machine.
2. Create and configure `src/.env` file from `src/.env.example`.
3. Run this command to start the application.
```shell
# install dependencies
cd src

# first time use
docker-compose up --build -d

## next time usage
docker-compose up -d
```

4. Open application in [http://localhost](http://localhost).

## Docker Configuration
- PHP 8.3
- PostgreSQL 13
- MongoDB 4.4
- MeiliSearch

## Resources
- Use [Laravel 10+ documentation](https://laravel.com/docs/10.x/eloquent) for Laravel usage guide.
- Use [Laravel Scout documentation](https://laravel.com/docs/10.x/scout) for MeiliSearch usage guide.
- Use [Prosper Technical documentation](https://premiro.getoutline.com/collection/prosper-ds3HbfjPDt) for Business Flow and Requirement.
- Use [MongoDB Laravel documentation](https://www.mongodb.com/docs/drivers/php/laravel-mongodb/v4.x/)

title: Prosper - Insurance Management Application
tags: [project, overview]
created: 2026-05-13
updated: 2026-05-19
---

# Prosper - Insurance Management Application

## What is Prosper?

Prosper is a Laravel 12 + Vue 3 (Inertia.js) insurance management application for handling insurance closings, endorsements, and DNCN (Debit Note / Credit Note) workflows.

## Tech Stack

| Layer | Technology |
|-------|------------|
| **Backend** | PHP 8.3+, Laravel 12 |
| **Frontend** | Vue 3, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, Inertia.js |
| **Database** | PostgreSQL (primary), MongoDB |
| **Queue** | Redis via Horizon |
| **Storage** | Aliyun OSS |
| **Testing** | PHPUnit |
| **Code Quality** | Laravel Pint, PHPStan (level 6), Rector |

## Key Architecture Decisions

### Two-Layer Architecture
- **BaseCrud / BaseWebCrud** — API Resources (Laravel `JsonResource` classes)
- **BaseCrudDto / BaseWebCrudDto** — Spatie DTOs

Controllers must extend the correct hierarchy based on their data layer.

### Authentication & Authorization
- **Roles**: `manager`, `finance`, `ceo` (global), staff
- **Teams**: Users belong to one or more teams via `user_teams`
- **Gating**: Team-scoped actions check user's team against record's `team` field

### Data Flow

```
User (Vue/TypeScript) 
  → Inertia Request 
    → Controller (extends BaseWebCrudDto)
      → DTO Validation (CreateClosingDto)
        → Model (Closing)
          → Resource DTO (ClosingDto)
            → JSON Response
```

### Task & Session Tracking

Work is tracked outside the codebase:

| Location | Purpose |
|----------|---------|
| `/Users/tnkapdy/Downloads/work/chats/prosper/TASKS.md` | Master task tracker |
| `/Users/tnkapdy/Downloads/work/chats/prosper/tasks/` | Individual task files with step-by-step plans |
| `/Users/tnkapdy/Downloads/work/chats/prosper/sessions/` | Session notes with analysis and decisions |
| `~/Downloads/work/notes/Prosper/` | Obsidian vault for knowledge base |

### AI Workflow Skills

Three specialized skills are configured for the AI workflow:

| Skill | Role | Output |
|-------|------|--------|
| **Architect** | Analyze, design, plan | Builder handoff prompt |
| **Builder** | Execute, implement | Code Reviewer handoff prompt |
| **Code Reviewer** | Verify, approve/reject | Architect re-plan prompt (if rejected) |

The chain loops: Architect → Builder → Reviewer → (back to Architect if issues found).

## Related Notes

- [[_index]] — Knowledge base index
- [[Getting Started]] — Setup and running the app
- [[Closing]] — Closing workflow, certificate sync, approval
- [[Sessions/2026-05-13]] — First session log
- [[Sessions/2026-05-18]] — Code review & certificate fixes
- [[Sessions/2026-05-19]] — Certificate form submission & sync fixes

---

*This vault is for personal reference and documentation. See AGENTS.md in the project repo for the authoritative developer guide.*

title: Closing Workflow
tags: [workflow, closing, insurance]
created: 2026-05-13
updated: 2026-05-19
---

# Closing Workflow

A **Closing** represents an insurance policy closing — the finalization of an insurance deal with all its details (insured, insurers, premium, brokerage, files, policies, certificates).

## Lifecycle

```
Create → Save (sync policies + certificates) → Approve → Read-only → Generate DNCN
```

### 1. Create

User fills out the closing form with:
- Policy details (policy number, cover note, period)
- Insured information
- Insurance type selection
- Team assignment
- Premium, brokerage, costs
- Insurer allocations
- Supporting files
- **Certificate numbers** (only for MC00 insurance type)

### 2. Save

When saving a closing:

1. **Insurers** are synced via `ClosingInsurer` records
2. **Subbrokerages** are synced via `ClosingSubbrokerage` records
3. **Policies** are synced via `syncPolicies()` method:
   - Creates/updates `Policy` records for each policy number in `policy_numbers_array`
4. **Certificates** are synced via `syncCertificates()` method:
   - Only runs for `MC00` insurance type
   - Reads from `certificate_numbers_array` in the request body
   - Looks up existing certificates by `uuid`/`id` first (for modal-created certs)
   - Falls back to orphan lookup (`whereNull('closing_id')` + `created_by`)
   - Creates new certificate if nothing found
   - Deletes certificates no longer in the list
   - Updates closing's `certificate_numbers` field

### Certificate Data Flow

```
User creates cert via modal → certificateApi.store() → DB (closing_id=null)
User adds cert to list → syncCertificateNumbers() → formData.certificate_numbers_array
User clicks Save → POST closings/store → __prepareDataStore() → afterStore()
    → syncCertificates() reads certificate_numbers_array → links certs to closing
```

### Certificate Number Generation

Certificate numbers are only generated for **MC00** (Marine Cargo) insurance type. Two paths:

| Path | When | How |
|------|------|-----|
| Modal create | User clicks "Create New Certificate" | `certificateApi.store()` → backend generates via `SequenceNumberGenerator` |
| Form save | User clicks Save on closing form | `syncCertificates()` → generates for any entry missing `certificate_number` |

### Certificate Form Fields

| Field | In formData? | Purpose |
|-------|-------------|---------|
| `certificate_numbers` | ✅ Yes | Comma-separated string of cert numbers (display only) |
| `certificate_numbers_array` | ✅ Yes | Array of cert objects with `policy_number`, `certificate_number`, `id`, `uuid` |

### 3. Approve

Manager with matching team can approve a closing:
- Sets `id_approval` to current user
- Sets `approval_time`
- Creates DNCN record via `DncnRepository::createDncn()`

After approval:
- All form fields become read-only (`:disabled` on individual inputs)
- Edit button and create new buttons are hidden
- Green banner shows "This closing has been approved and is now read-only."

### 4. Read-only

Approved closings cannot be edited. Attempting to update throws `ClosingApprovedException`.

### 5. Generate DNCN

After approval, DN/CN can be generated:
- Requires `id_approval` to be set
- Blocks if DNCN already exists
- Creates records via `DncnRepository::createDncn()`

---

## Key Files

### Backend

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `app/Http/Controllers/Web/Transaction/ClosingController.php` | Main CRUD controller with `syncPolicies()` and `syncCertificates()` |
| `app/Http/Controllers/Api/Transaction/CertificateController.php` | API for certificate CRUD — `generate()`, `store()`, `update()` |
| `app/Models/Transaction/Closing.php` | Closing model with `$fillable`, relationships |
| `app/Models/Transaction/Certificate.php` | Certificate model with `HasBaseOwner` trait |
| `app/DTOs/Requests/CreateClosingDto.php` | Validation and DTO for create |
| `app/DTOs/Resources/ClosingDto.php` | Resource DTO for output |
| `app/Exceptions/ClosingApprovedException.php` | Exception thrown when editing approved closing |
| `app/Services/SequenceNumberGenerator.php` | Generates certificate numbers |

### Frontend

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `resources/js/Pages/Transaction/Closing/Tabs/FormClosing/Index.vue` | Main form page, `onSubmit`, formData definition |
| `resources/js/Pages/Transaction/Closing/Tabs/FormClosing/LeftForm.vue` | Left section — policy & certificate management |
| `resources/js/Pages/Transaction/Closing/Tabs/FormClosing/RightForm.vue` | Right section — financial details |
| `resources/js/apis/certificate.api.ts` | Certificate API definitions via Ziggy |
| `resources/js/types/closing.ts` | TypeScript types including `Certificate` interface |

---

## Error Handling

When attempting to edit an approved closing:

1. `__beforeUpdate()` in `ClosingController` throws `ClosingApprovedException`
2. Exception handler returns 422 JSON with:
   ```json
   {
     "message": "This closing has been approved and cannot be edited.",
     "status": "closing_approved",
     "errors": {}
   }
   ```
3. Frontend detects `status === 'closing_approved'` and refreshes the page to show read-only state

---

## Team Support

- **Single team user**: Team auto-selected on mount
- **Multi-team user**: Dropdown to select team
- Team is stored as string (`team` column) + foreign key (`team_id`)
- Approval and actions are gated by team matching

---

## Known Issues & Gotchas

| Issue | Status | Notes |
|-------|--------|-------|
| `generate()` was persisting + `store()` was persisting = double-create | ✅ Fixed | `generate()` now returns number only |
| `certificate_numbers_array` not in formData → not in request body | ✅ Fixed | Added to formData definition in Index.vue |
| `syncCertificates()` read from `policy_numbers_array` instead of `certificate_numbers_array` | ⏳ Pending | Task 008 — rewrite to read correct source |
| API response not unwrapped correctly in `submitNewCertificate()` | ✅ Fixed | `created?.data?.id` instead of `created?.id` |
| `$leader` null pointer if no insurers exist | ✅ Fixed | Added null guard |
| Orphan lookup not scoped to user | ✅ Fixed | Added `where('created_by', Auth::id())` |

---

- [[Sessions/2026-05-13]] — Certificate number gating session
- [[Sessions/2026-05-18]] — Code review & certificate module fixes

title: "2026-05-19 — Certificate Form Submission & Sync Fixes"
tags: [session, closing, certificate, bug-fix]
date: 2026-05-19
---

# 2026-05-19 — Certificate Form Submission & Sync Fixes

## Objective

Fix certificates not being included in the closing form request body, and fix `syncCertificates()` reading from the wrong data source.

## Issue 1: Certificate Array Not in Request Body (Task 007)

### Problem

`certificate_numbers` and `certificate_numbers_array` were set on `formData` in `LeftForm.vue` but never serialized into the Inertia POST request because they weren't defined in the `formData` definition in `Index.vue`.

### Root Cause

```
policy_numbers_array → defined in formData (Index.vue:148) → ✅ in request
certificate_numbers_array → NOT defined in formData → ❌ NOT in request
```

### Fix

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `Index.vue` | Added `certificate_numbers` and `certificate_numbers_array` to `formData` definition |
| `Index.vue` | Added `Certificate` type import and `certificate_numbers_array` to `FormClosing` interface |
| `ClosingController.php` | Added `certificate_numbers` and `certificate_numbers_array` to `unset_array` |
| `LeftForm.vue` | Added `id` and `uuid` to `certificate_numbers_array` mapping |

## Issue 2: syncCertificates Reads Wrong Data Source (Task 008)

### Problem

`syncCertificates()` reads from `policy_numbers_array` but certificates created via "Create New Certificate" modal are stored in `certificate_numbers_array` — two completely separate data sources.

### Data Flow

```
Policy inline rows → syncPolicyNumbers() → policy_numbers_array
Certificate modal entries → syncCertificateNumbers() → certificate_numbers_array

syncCertificates() reads: policy_numbers_array ❌ WRONG SOURCE
syncCertificates() should read: certificate_numbers_array ✅ CORRECT SOURCE
```

### Fix

| Change | Reason |
|--------|--------|
| Read from `certificate_numbers_array` | Correct data source — contains modal-created certificates |
| Lookup by `uuid`/`id` first | Modal certs have `id`/`uuid` — direct lookup is more reliable |
| Fall back to orphan lookup | Handles edge case where modal cert wasn't saved properly |
| Remove `dd('here')` | Leftover debug statement that crashes the app |

## Issue 3: Certificate Entry Data Unwrapping (Task 006)

### Problem

`submitNewCertificate()` read `new_certificate.certificate_number` (blank form input) instead of the API response value. `API_SERVICE` is raw axios with no interceptor unwrap.

### Fix

Changed `created?.id` → `created?.data?.id` and `new_certificate.certificate_number` → `created?.data?.certificate_number` in `LeftForm.vue`.

## Task Tracker

| ID | Title | Status |
|----|-------|--------|
| 006 | Fix Certificate Entry Data Unwrapping After Store | ✅ Completed |
| 007 | Add Certificate Array to Closing Form Submission | ✅ Completed |
| 008 | Fix syncCertificates to Read certificate_numbers_array | ⏳ Pending |

## Related Notes

- [[Closing]] — Certificate sync workflow updated
- [[Sessions/2026-05-18]] — Previous certificate module fixes

# Prosper - Insurance Management Application

## What is Prosper?

Prosper is a Laravel 12 + Vue 3 (Inertia.js) insurance management application for handling insurance closings, endorsements, and DNCN (Debit Note / Credit Note) workflows.

## Tech Stack

| Layer | Technology |
|-------|------------|
| **Backend** | PHP 8.3+, Laravel 12 |
| **Frontend** | Vue 3, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, Inertia.js |
| **Database** | PostgreSQL (primary), MongoDB |
| **Queue** | Redis via Horizon |
| **Storage** | Aliyun OSS |
| **Testing** | PHPUnit |
| **Code Quality** | Laravel Pint, PHPStan (level 6), Rector |

## Key Architecture Decisions

### Two-Layer Architecture
- **BaseCrud / BaseWebCrud** — API Resources (Laravel `JsonResource` classes)
- **BaseCrudDto / BaseWebCrudDto** — Spatie DTOs

Controllers must extend the correct hierarchy based on their data layer.

### Authentication & Authorization
- **Roles**: `manager`, `finance`, `ceo` (global), staff
- **Teams**: Users belong to one or more teams via `user_teams`
- **Gating**: Team-scoped actions check user's team against record's `team` field

### Data Flow

```
User (Vue/TypeScript) 
  → Inertia Request 
    → Controller (extends BaseWebCrudDto)
      → DTO Validation (CreateClosingDto)
        → Model (Closing)
          → Resource DTO (ClosingDto)
            → JSON Response
```

### Task & Session Tracking

Work is tracked outside the codebase:

| Location | Purpose |
|----------|---------|
| `/Users/tnkapdy/Downloads/work/chats/prosper/TASKS.md` | Master task tracker |
| `/Users/tnkapdy/Downloads/work/chats/prosper/tasks/` | Individual task files with step-by-step plans |
| `/Users/tnkapdy/Downloads/work/chats/prosper/sessions/` | Session notes with analysis and decisions |
| `~/Downloads/work/notes/Prosper/` | Obsidian vault for knowledge base |

### AI Workflow Skills

Three specialized skills are configured for the AI workflow:

| Skill | Role | Output |
|-------|------|--------|
| **Architect** | Analyze, design, plan | Builder handoff prompt |
| **Builder** | Execute, implement | Code Reviewer handoff prompt |
| **Code Reviewer** | Verify, approve/reject | Architect re-plan prompt (if rejected) |

The chain loops: Architect → Builder → Reviewer → (back to Architect if issues found).

## Related Notes

- [[_index]] — Knowledge base index
- [[Getting Started]] — Setup and running the app
- [[Closing]] — Closing workflow, certificate sync, approval
- [[Sessions/2026-05-13]] — First session log
- [[Sessions/2026-05-18]] — Code review & certificate fixes
- [[Sessions/2026-05-19]] — Certificate form submission & sync fixes

title: Closing Workflow
tags: [workflow, closing, insurance]
created: 2026-05-13
updated: 2026-05-19
---

# Closing Workflow

A **Closing** represents an insurance policy closing — the finalization of an insurance deal with all its details (insured, insurers, premium, brokerage, files, policies, certificates).

## Lifecycle

```
Create → Save (sync policies + certificates) → Approve → Read-only → Generate DNCN
```

### 1. Create

User fills out the closing form with:
- Policy details (policy number, cover note, period)
- Insured information
- Insurance type selection
- Team assignment
- Premium, brokerage, costs
- Insurer allocations
- Supporting files
- **Certificate numbers** (only for MC00 insurance type)

### 2. Save

When saving a closing:

1. **Insurers** are synced via `ClosingInsurer` records
2. **Subbrokerages** are synced via `ClosingSubbrokerage` records
3. **Policies** are synced via `syncPolicies()` method:
   - Creates/updates `Policy` records for each policy number in `policy_numbers_array`
4. **Certificates** are synced via `syncCertificates()` method:
   - Only runs for `MC00` insurance type
   - Reads from `certificate_numbers_array` in the request body
   - Looks up existing certificates by `uuid`/`id` first (for modal-created certs)
   - Falls back to orphan lookup (`whereNull('closing_id')` + `created_by`)
   - Creates new certificate if nothing found
   - Deletes certificates no longer in the list
   - Updates closing's `certificate_numbers` field

### Certificate Data Flow

```
User creates cert via modal → certificateApi.store() → DB (closing_id=null)
User adds cert to list → syncCertificateNumbers() → formData.certificate_numbers_array
User clicks Save → POST closings/store → __prepareDataStore() → afterStore()
    → syncCertificates() reads certificate_numbers_array → links certs to closing
```

### Certificate Number Generation

Certificate numbers are only generated for **MC00** (Marine Cargo) insurance type. Two paths:

| Path | When | How |
|------|------|-----|
| Modal create | User clicks "Create New Certificate" | `certificateApi.store()` → backend generates via `SequenceNumberGenerator` |
| Form save | User clicks Save on closing form | `syncCertificates()` → generates for any entry missing `certificate_number` |

### Certificate Form Fields

| Field | In formData? | Purpose |
|-------|-------------|---------|
| `certificate_numbers` | ✅ Yes | Comma-separated string of cert numbers (display only) |
| `certificate_numbers_array` | ✅ Yes | Array of cert objects with `policy_number`, `certificate_number`, `id`, `uuid` |

### 3. Approve

Manager with matching team can approve a closing:
- Sets `id_approval` to current user
- Sets `approval_time`
- Creates DNCN record via `DncnRepository::createDncn()`

After approval:
- All form fields become read-only (`:disabled` on individual inputs)
- Edit button and create new buttons are hidden
- Green banner shows "This closing has been approved and is now read-only."

### 4. Read-only

Approved closings cannot be edited. Attempting to update throws `ClosingApprovedException`.

### 5. Generate DNCN

After approval, DN/CN can be generated:
- Requires `id_approval` to be set
- Blocks if DNCN already exists
- Creates records via `DncnRepository::createDncn()`

---

## Key Files

### Backend

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `app/Http/Controllers/Web/Transaction/ClosingController.php` | Main CRUD controller with `syncPolicies()` and `syncCertificates()` |
| `app/Http/Controllers/Api/Transaction/CertificateController.php` | API for certificate CRUD — `generate()`, `store()`, `update()` |
| `app/Models/Transaction/Closing.php` | Closing model with `$fillable`, relationships |
| `app/Models/Transaction/Certificate.php` | Certificate model with `HasBaseOwner` trait |
| `app/DTOs/Requests/CreateClosingDto.php` | Validation and DTO for create |
| `app/DTOs/Resources/ClosingDto.php` | Resource DTO for output |
| `app/Exceptions/ClosingApprovedException.php` | Exception thrown when editing approved closing |
| `app/Services/SequenceNumberGenerator.php` | Generates certificate numbers |

### Frontend

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `resources/js/Pages/Transaction/Closing/Tabs/FormClosing/Index.vue` | Main form page, `onSubmit`, formData definition |
| `resources/js/Pages/Transaction/Closing/Tabs/FormClosing/LeftForm.vue` | Left section — policy & certificate management |
| `resources/js/Pages/Transaction/Closing/Tabs/FormClosing/RightForm.vue` | Right section — financial details |
| `resources/js/apis/certificate.api.ts` | Certificate API definitions via Ziggy |
| `resources/js/types/closing.ts` | TypeScript types including `Certificate` interface |

---

## Error Handling

When attempting to edit an approved closing:

1. `__beforeUpdate()` in `ClosingController` throws `ClosingApprovedException`
2. Exception handler returns 422 JSON with:
   ```json
   {
     "message": "This closing has been approved and cannot be edited.",
     "status": "closing_approved",
     "errors": {}
   }
   ```
3. Frontend detects `status === 'closing_approved'` and refreshes the page to show read-only state

---

## Team Support

- **Single team user**: Team auto-selected on mount
- **Multi-team user**: Dropdown to select team
- Team is stored as string (`team` column) + foreign key (`team_id`)
- Approval and actions are gated by team matching

---

## Known Issues & Gotchas

| Issue | Status | Notes |
|-------|--------|-------|
| `generate()` was persisting + `store()` was persisting = double-create | ✅ Fixed | `generate()` now returns number only |
| `certificate_numbers_array` not in formData → not in request body | ✅ Fixed | Added to formData definition in Index.vue |
| `syncCertificates()` read from `policy_numbers_array` instead of `certificate_numbers_array` | ⏳ Pending | Task 008 — rewrite to read correct source |
| API response not unwrapped correctly in `submitNewCertificate()` | ✅ Fixed | `created?.data?.id` instead of `created?.id` |
| `$leader` null pointer if no insurers exist | ✅ Fixed | Added null guard |
| Orphan lookup not scoped to user | ✅ Fixed | Added `where('created_by', Auth::id())` |

---

## Related Notes

- [[_index]] — Knowledge base index
- [[Overview]] — Project overview and tech stack
- [[Getting Started]] — Setup and commands
- [[Sessions/2026-05-13]] — Certificate number gating session
- [[Sessions/2026-05-18]] — Code review & certificate module fixes
- [[Sessions/2026-05-19]] — Certificate form submission & sync fixes

| `Index.vue` | Added `certificate_numbers` and `certificate_numbers_array` to `formData` definition |
| `Index.vue` | Added `Certificate` type import and `certificate_numbers_array` to `FormClosing` interface |
| `ClosingController.php` | Added `certificate_numbers` and `certificate_numbers_array` to `unset_array` |
| `LeftForm.vue` | Added `id` and `uuid` to `certificate_numbers_array` mapping |

## Issue 2: syncCertificates Reads Wrong Data Source (Task 008)

### Problem

`syncCertificates()` reads from `policy_numbers_array` but certificates created via "Create New Certificate" modal are stored in `certificate_numbers_array` — two completely separate data sources.

### Data Flow

```
Policy inline rows → syncPolicyNumbers() → policy_numbers_array
Certificate modal entries → syncCertificateNumbers() → certificate_numbers_array

syncCertificates() reads: policy_numbers_array ❌ WRONG SOURCE
syncCertificates() should read: certificate_numbers_array ✅ CORRECT SOURCE
```

### Fix

| Change | Reason |
|--------|--------|
| Read from `certificate_numbers_array` | Correct data source — contains modal-created certificates |
| Lookup by `uuid`/`id` first | Modal certs have `id`/`uuid` — direct lookup is more reliable |
| Fall back to orphan lookup | Handles edge case where modal cert wasn't saved properly |
| Remove `dd('here')` | Leftover debug statement that crashes the app |

## Issue 3: Certificate Entry Data Unwrapping (Task 006)

### Problem

`submitNewCertificate()` read `new_certificate.certificate_number` (blank form input) instead of the API response value. `API_SERVICE` is raw axios with no interceptor unwrap.

### Fix

Changed `created?.id` → `created?.data?.id` and `new_certificate.certificate_number` → `created?.data?.certificate_number` in `LeftForm.vue`.

## Task Tracker

| ID | Title | Status |
|----|-------|--------|
| 006 | Fix Certificate Entry Data Unwrapping After Store | ✅ Completed |
| 007 | Add Certificate Array to Closing Form Submission | ✅ Completed |
| 008 | Fix syncCertificates to Read certificate_numbers_array | ⏳ Pending |

## Related Notes

- [[Closing]] — Certificate sync workflow updated
- [[Sessions/2026-05-18]] — Previous certificate module fixes

content.data: Now let me read the existing .kiro files to understand what's already there:

# Prosper - Insurance Brokerage System

## Stack
- **Backend**: Laravel 12, PHP 8.3, Sanctum auth, Inertia.js
- **Frontend**: Vue 3 (Composition API, `<script setup>`), TypeScript, Pinia, Tailwind CSS 3
- **Database**: PostgreSQL (primary), MongoDB (secondary), Redis (cache/queue)
- **Search**: Meilisearch via Laravel Scout
- **Queue**: Laravel Horizon + Kafka
- **Storage**: Aliyun OSS, Google Drive
- **Tools**: PHPStan (level 6), Pint (Laravel preset + strict rules), Rector, Vite

## Environment

All commands run inside Docker — PHP and Node.js are NOT on the host.

```bash
# Container name
docker exec prosper_app_dev <cmd>

# Makefile shortcuts
make up                    # Build & start containers + Meilisearch setup
make down                  # Stop containers
make logs                  # Tail app logs
make health                # Check /health endpoint
make npm cmd="install"     # Run npm commands
make npm cmd="run build"   # Build frontend
```

## Commands

```bash
# Lint & Analysis
docker exec prosper_app_dev ./vendor/bin/pint
docker exec prosper_app_dev ./vendor/bin/phpstan analyse
docker exec prosper_app_dev ./vendor/bin/rector

# Test
docker exec prosper_app_dev ./vendor/bin/phpunit
docker exec prosper_app_dev ./vendor/bin/phpunit --filter=test_name
docker exec prosper_app_dev ./vendor/bin/phpunit tests/Feature/SomeTest.php

# Artisan
docker exec prosper_app_dev php artisan migrate
docker exec prosper_app_dev php artisan db:seed --class=SomeSeeder

# Frontend (Vite dev server runs automatically in container)
docker exec prosper_app_dev npm run build
```

## Architecture

Inertia.js monolith — backend renders via Inertia, frontend is Vue SPA.

```
src/
├── app/
│   ├── Http/Controllers/{Web,Api}/   # Thin controllers
│   ├── Http/Repositories/            # Data access layer
│   ├── Models/{Transaction,Claim,Master,Config}/ # Domain-grouped models
│   ├── Services/                     # Business logic + BaseCrud hierarchies
│   ├── DTOs/{Resources,Requests}/    # Spatie Data DTOs
│   ├── Enums/                        # PHP-backed enums
│   ├── Actions/                      # Single-purpose action classes
│   ├── Policies/                     # Authorization
│   └── Traits/                       # HasBaseOwner, HasBaseTable, etc.
├── resources/js/
│   ├── apis/                         # Ziggy route-based API definitions
│   ├── Components/                   # Reusable Vue components
│   ├── Pages/{Module}/{Name}/        # Inertia pages
│   ├── stores/                       # Pinia stores
│   ├── types/                        # TypeScript interfaces (per domain)
│   ├── utils/                        # Helpers, apiservice, useapiform
│   └── i18n/{module}/{locale}.ts     # vue-i18n translations
├── database/
│   ├── migrations/{Module}/          # Grouped by domain
│   └── seeders/OneTime/              # One-time data migration seeders
└── routes/{Web,Api}/{Module}/route.php
```

## CRUD Base Classes

Two hierarchies — choose based on data layer:

| Hierarchy | Use when | Properties |
|-----------|----------|------------|
| `BaseCrud` → `BaseWebCrud` | Using Laravel `JsonResource` | `$resource`, `$storeValidator`, `$updateValidator` |
| `BaseCrudDto` → `BaseWebCrudDto` | Using Spatie Data DTOs | `$resourceDto`, `$storeDto`, `$updateDto` |

All properties have native PHP types. Child controllers MUST include matching types (PHPStan enforces).

## Auth & Roles

| Role | Scope | Permissions |
|------|-------|-------------|
| `manager` | Team-scoped | Approve closings, manager-level DNCN approval |
| `finance` | Team `finance` | Finance-level DNCN approval/unapproval |
| `ceo` | Global | Director-level approval; can skip stages |
| (staff) | Team-scoped | Create/edit closings and endorsements |

**Shared Inertia data** (via `HandleInertiaRequests.php`):
- `auth.teams` — user's teams array
- `auth.role` — user's primary role name

**Team-gated pattern**:
```php
// Backend policy
$user->hasRole('manager') && $user->teams->contains('name', $closing->team);
```
```ts
// Frontend computed
auth.role === 'manager' && auth.teams.some(t => t.name === data.team)
```

## Conventions

### PHP
- `declare(strict_types=1)` in every file
- Pint enforces: strict comparison, ordered class elements, no useless else, mb_str_functions
- Use `$request->validated()` or `$request->safe()` — never `$request->all()`
- Eager-load relationships to avoid N+1
- Spatie Data objects for complex data transfer
- Models with `HasBaseOwner` must include 6 base fields in `$fillable`: `created_at`, `updated_at`, `deleted_at`, `created_by`, `updated_by`, `deleted_by`

### Vue/TypeScript
- `<script setup lang="ts">` only — no Options API
- Never use `any` — define proper types
- Path aliases: `@` → `resources/js/`, `@assets` → `resources/assets/`
- Pinia stores: `useXxxStore()` pattern
- API files: `resources/js/apis/{name}.api.ts` using Ziggy routes
- Types: one file per domain in `resources/js/types/`
- Components: PascalCase filenames
- Icons: Lucide via `lucide-vue-next`
- Tables: Tabulator, Dropdowns: Tom Select, Dates: Litepicker

### Naming
- Controllers: `PascalCaseController`
- Models: Singular PascalCase
- Routes: `module.resources.action` (snake_case)
- DB tables: snake_case plural
- Methods: camelCase
- Constants: UPPER_SNAKE_CASE

## Deployment

```bash
make env=prod deploy    # Zero-downtime: pull → migrate → restart → meilisearch → health check
```

Branch strategy: `feature/*` → PR to `main`. Never push directly to `main`.

---
name: prosper-patterns
description: Project-specific patterns for Prosper insurance brokerage system. Use when writing or reviewing code in this project.
---

# Prosper Patterns

## Controller Pattern
Thin controllers that delegate to services/repositories:
```php
public function index(Request $request): Response
{
    $data = $this->repository->paginate($request);
    return Inertia::render('PageName/Index', ['data' => $data]);
}
```

## Repository Pattern
Repositories in `app/Http/Repositories/` handle data access:
- Return paginated results for index pages
- Use eager loading for relationships
- Use Spatie Data DTOs for complex transformations

## Service Pattern
Services in `app/Services/` handle business logic:
- Injected via constructor
- Handle multi-step operations in DB transactions
- Dispatch events/jobs for side effects

## API Layer (Frontend)
Each domain has an API file in `resources/js/apis/`:
```ts
// resources/js/apis/domain.api.ts
import { apiService } from '@/utils/apiservice'
export const domainApi = {
  index: (params) => apiService.get('/api/domain', { params }),
  store: (data) => apiService.post('/api/domain', data),
}
```

## Page Structure (Inertia)
```
Pages/
├── Domain/
│   ├── Index.vue      # List view with Tabulator table
│   ├── Form.vue       # Create/Edit form
│   └── Show.vue       # Detail view (if needed)
```

## Type Definitions
All TypeScript interfaces in `resources/js/types/`:
- One file per domain (e.g., `claim.ts`, `master.ts`, `voucher.ts`)
- Shared types in `shared.ts`

## i18n
Translation files per domain in `resources/js/i18n/{domain}/`:
- `en.ts` and `th.ts` (or other languages)

items.Text:
# Prosper - Insurance Brokerage System

## Stack
- **Backend**: Laravel 12, PHP 8.3, Sanctum auth, Inertia.js
- **Frontend**: Vue 3 (Composition API, `<script setup>`), TypeScript, Pinia, Tailwind CSS 3
- **Database**: PostgreSQL (primary), MongoDB (secondary), Redis (cache/queue)
- **Search**: Meilisearch via Laravel Scout
- **Queue**: Laravel Horizon + Kafka
- **Storage**: Aliyun OSS, Google Drive
- **Tools**: PHPStan (level 6), Pint (Laravel preset + strict rules), Rector, Vite

## Environment

All commands run inside Docker — PHP and Node.js are NOT on the host.

```bash
# Container name
docker exec prosper_app_dev <cmd>

# Makefile shortcuts
make up                    # Build & start containers + Meilisearch setup
make down                  # Stop containers
make logs                  # Tail app logs
make health                # Check /health endpoint
make npm cmd="install"     # Run npm commands
make npm cmd="run build"   # Build frontend
```

## Commands

```bash
# Lint & Analysis
docker exec prosper_app_dev ./vendor/bin/pint
docker exec prosper_app_dev ./vendor/bin/phpstan analyse
docker exec prosper_app_dev ./vendor/bin/rector

# Test
docker exec prosper_app_dev ./vendor/bin/phpunit
docker exec prosper_app_dev ./vendor/bin/phpunit --filter=test_name
docker exec prosper_app_dev ./vendor/bin/phpunit tests/Feature/SomeTest.php

# Artisan
docker exec prosper_app_dev php artisan migrate
docker exec prosper_app_dev php artisan db:seed --class=SomeSeeder

# Frontend (Vite dev server runs automatically in container)
docker exec prosper_app_dev npm run build
```

## Architecture

Inertia.js monolith — backend renders via Inertia, frontend is Vue SPA.

```
src/
├── app/
│   ├── Http/Controllers/{Web,Api}/   # Thin controllers
│   ├── Http/Repositories/            # Data access layer
│   ├── Models/{Transaction,Claim,Master,Config}/ # Domain-grouped models
│   ├── Services/                     # Business logic + BaseCrud hierarchies
│   ├── DTOs/{Resources,Requests}/    # Spatie Data DTOs
│   ├── Enums/                        # PHP-backed enums
│   ├── Actions/                      # Single-purpose action classes
│   ├── Policies/                     # Authorization
│   └── Traits/                       # HasBaseOwner, HasBaseTable, etc.
├── resources/js/
│   ├── apis/                         # Ziggy route-based API definitions
│   ├── Components/                   # Reusable Vue components
│   ├── Pages/{Module}/{Name}/        # Inertia pages
│   ├── stores/                       # Pinia stores
│   ├── types/                        # TypeScript interfaces (per domain)
│   ├── utils/                        # Helpers, apiservice, useapiform
│   └── i18n/{module}/{locale}.ts     # vue-i18n translations
├── database/
│   ├── migrations/{Module}/          # Grouped by domain
│   └── seeders/OneTime/              # One-time data migration seeders
└── routes/{Web,Api}/{Module}/route.php
```

## CRUD Base Classes

Two hierarchies — choose based on data layer:

| Hierarchy | Use when | Properties |
|-----------|----------|------------|
| `BaseCrud` → `BaseWebCrud` | Using Laravel `JsonResource` | `$resource`, `$storeValidator`, `$updateValidator` |
| `BaseCrudDto` → `BaseWebCrudDto` | Using Spatie Data DTOs | `$resourceDto`, `$storeDto`, `$updateDto` |

All properties have native PHP types. Child controllers MUST include matching types (PHPStan enforces).

## Auth & Roles

| Role | Scope | Permissions |
|------|-------|-------------|
| `manager` | Team-scoped | Approve closings, manager-level DNCN approval |
| `finance` | Team `finance` | Finance-level DNCN approval/unapproval |
| `ceo` | Global | Director-level approval; can skip stages |
| (staff) | Team-scoped | Create/edit closings and endorsements |

**Shared Inertia data** (via `HandleInertiaRequests.php`):
- `auth.teams` — user's teams array
- `auth.role` — user's primary role name

**Team-gated pattern**:
```php
// Backend policy
$user->hasRole('manager') && $user->teams->contains('name', $closing->team);
```
```ts
// Frontend computed
auth.role === 'manager' && auth.teams.some(t => t.name === data.team)
```

## Conventions

### PHP
- `declare(strict_types=1)` in every file
- Pint enforces: strict comparison, ordered class elements, no useless else, mb_str_functions
- Use `$request->validated()` or `$request->safe()` — never `$request->all()`
- Eager-load relationships to avoid N+1
- Spatie Data objects for complex data transfer
- Models with `HasBaseOwner` must include 6 base fields in `$fillable`: `created_at`, `updated_at`, `deleted_at`, `created_by`, `updated_by`, `deleted_by`

### Vue/TypeScript
- `<script setup lang="ts">` only — no Options API
- Never use `any` — define proper types
- Path aliases: `@` → `resources/js/`, `@assets` → `resources/assets/`
- Pinia stores: `useXxxStore()` pattern
- API files: `resources/js/apis/{name}.api.ts` using Ziggy routes
- Types: one file per domain in `resources/js/types/`
- Components: PascalCase filenames
- Icons: Lucide via `lucide-vue-next`
- Tables: Tabulator, Dropdowns: Tom Select, Dates: Litepicker

### Naming
- Controllers: `PascalCaseController`
- Models: Singular PascalCase
- Routes: `module.resources.action` (snake_case)
- DB tables: snake_case plural
- Methods: camelCase
- Constants: UPPER_SNAKE_CASE

## Deployment

```bash
make env=prod deploy    # Zero-downtime: pull → migrate → restart → meilisearch → health check
```

Branch strategy: `feature/*` → PR to `main`. Never push directly to `main`.

Done. Here's what was created/updated:

**`.kiro/settings.json`** — Project metadata, important files list, exclude patterns, and Docker commands for lint/analyse/test/build.

**`.kiro/skills/prosper-patterns/SKILL.md`** — Comprehensive patterns guide including:
- Docker environment reminder
- CRUD controller pattern (BaseWebCrudDto with typed properties)
- Model pattern (HasBaseOwner + 6 base fields)
- DTO patterns (Resource + Request)
- Frontend patterns (API, Page, TypeScript interfaces, i18n)
- Routes pattern
- Known gotchas table (from AGENTS.md)
- Auth & team gating patterns
- Full 12-step CRUD checklist for new resources

The existing `steering.md` was already solid so I left it as-is. The SKILL.md now serves as the actionable reference that gets loaded when writing code in this project.

data.documentation:
--- Documentation for: token usage billing cost tracking ---
The following documentation is provided inline. DO NOT attempt to read files - all content is below.



--- Document 1 ---
--- doc_meta: validated: 2026-04-09 commit: 4ae084db status: validated testable_headless: false category: slash_command title: /usage description: Show account-level usage limits and subscription plan information keywords: [usage, billing, credits, plan, limits, subscription] related: [model, settings] --- # /usage Show account-level usage limits and subscription plan information. ## Overview Retrieves your account's usage limits from the API, including subscription plan details, usage breakdowns by resource type, and bonus credits with expiry information. ## Usage ``` /usage ``` ## Output Returns JSON data containing: - `planName` - Your subscription plan title - `overagesEnabled` - Whether overage charges are enabled - `isEnterprise` - Whether this is an enterprise-managed plan - `usageBreakdowns[]` - Array of usage by resource type: - `resourceType`, `displayName` - `used`, `limit`, `percentage` - `currentOverages`, `overageRate`, `overageCharges`, `currency` - `bonusCredits[]` - Array of bonus credits: - `name`, `used`, `total`, `daysUntilExpiry` ## Limitations - Enterprise users see "Your plan is managed by admin" message - Requires valid API authentication ## Technical Details Calls `get_usage_limits()` API to retrieve account-level subscription and usage data. ## Examples ### Example 1: View Usage ``` /usage ``` **Output**: ``` Plan: Q Developer Pro | 2 usage breakdowns ``` With JSON data: ```json { "planName": "Q Developer Pro", "overagesEnabled": false, "isEnterprise": false, "usageBreakdowns": [ { "resourceType": "AGENTIC_REQUESTS", "displayName": "Agentic requests", "used": 150.0, "limit": 1000.0, "percentage": 15, "currentOverages": 0.0, "overageRate": 0.0, "overageCharges": null, "currency": "USD" } ], "bonusCredits": [ { "name": "Welcome bonus", "used": 10.0, "total": 50.0, "daysUntilExpiry": 25 } ] } ``` ## Troubleshooting ### Issue: "Your plan is managed by admin" **Symptom**: Message says plan is managed by admin **Cause**: Enterprise account with centrally managed billing **Solution**: Contact your organization's admin for usage details ### Issue: Failed to retrieve usage information **Symptom**: Error message about retrieval failure **Cause**: API authentication or connectivity issue **Solution**: Check your login status with `/whoami` and re-authenticate if needed

--- Document 2 ---
--- doc_meta: title: todo_list description: Task list tool for tracking multi-step work with create, complete, add, remove, and list commands category: tool keywords: [todo_list, todo, task, task_list, plan, checklist, tracking] related: [switch-to-execution] validated: 2026-04-08 commit: 1a984cb0 status: validated testable_headless: true --- ## Overview > This tool is used by the AI assistant to fulfill your requests. You don't invoke it directly - simply ask questions naturally. The task list tool creates and tracks multi-step tasks. It is used automatically when the assistant receives a task that requires multiple steps. Tasks are created before execution begins and marked off as they are completed. ## Usage The tool uses a `command` field to select the operation. ### create Create a new task list, replacing any existing one. - `tasks` (array, required) — List of tasks, each with: - `task_description` (string, required) — The task description - `details` (string, optional) — Additional details - `task_list_description` (string, required) — Brief summary of the overall task list ### complete Mark tasks as completed and record context. - `completed_task_ids` (array, required) — IDs of completed tasks - `context_update` (string, required) — Important context about what was done - `modified_files` (array, optional) — Paths of files modified during the task ### add Add new tasks to an existing task list. - `new_tasks` (array, required) — Tasks to add (same format as `create`) - `new_description` (string, optional) — Updated task list description if the goal changed ### remove Remove tasks from the list. - `remove_task_ids` (array, required) — IDs of tasks to remove - `new_description` (string, optional) — Updated task list description if the goal changed ### list Show all current tasks and their status. No parameters required. ## Examples ### Create a task list ```json { "command": "create", "task_list_description": "Add user authentication to the API", "tasks": [ {"task_description": "Create user model and migration"}, {"task_description": "Implement login endpoint"}, {"task_description": "Add JWT token generation"}, {"task_description": "Write tests for auth flow"} ] } ``` ### Mark a task complete ```json { "command": "complete", "completed_task_ids": ["1"], "context_update": "Created User model with email, password_hash fields. Migration adds users table.", "modified_files": ["src/models/user.rs", "migrations/001_create_users.sql"] } ``` ### Add tasks mid-execution ```json { "command": "add", "new_tasks": [ {"task_description": "Add rate limiting to login endpoint"} ], "new_description": "Add user authentication with rate limiting to the API" } ``` ## Troubleshooting ### "No tasks were provided" The `tasks` or `new_tasks` array is empty. Provide at least one task. ### "No task description was provided" The `task_list_description` is empty or missing for the `create` command. ### Task ID not found The ID in `completed_task_ids` or `remove_task_ids` doesn't match any existing task. Use the `list` command to see current task IDs. ## Related - [switch-to-execution](switch-to-execution.md) — Signals transition from planning to execution

--- Document 3 ---
--- doc_meta: validated: 2025-12-22 commit: 57090ffe status: validated testable_headless: false category: feature title: MCP Registry description: Enterprise MCP server security allowing administrators to control which servers users can access keywords: [mcp, registry, security, enterprise, admin, pro] related: [cmd-mcp, slash-mcp, agent-configuration] --- # MCP Registry Enterprise MCP server security allowing administrators to control which servers users can access. ## Overview Pro-tier customers using IAM Identity Center can have MCP server access controlled through an MCP registry. When configured by administrators, users can only use MCP servers explicitly allowed in the registry. Provides centralized security for enterprise deployments. ## How It Works **Without Registry** (default): - Users can add any MCP server - Servers configured in agent files or via CLI - No central control **With Registry** (enterprise): - Administrator configures allowed servers - Users select from registry list - Cannot add custom servers - Centralized security and security ## Adding Servers (Registry Mode) ### In Chat ``` /mcp add ``` Shows interactive list of servers from organization's registry. ### In CLI ```bash # Add specific server kiro-cli mcp add --name myserver # Add to workspace kiro-cli mcp add --scope workspace # Add to specific agent kiro-cli mcp add --agent myagent # Interactive selection kiro-cli mcp add ``` **Note**: Server name must match registry. Cannot add custom servers. ## Removing Servers ### In Chat ``` /mcp remove ``` Interactive menu to select server to remove. ### In CLI ```bash kiro-cli mcp remove --name <server-name> ``` ## Viewing Available Servers ### In Chat ``` /mcp list ``` Shows: - All locally configured MCP servers - Server status and configuration - Available tools from each server ## Customization (Registry Mode) Even with registry, you can customize: ### Local (stdio) Servers - Environment variables (API keys, paths) - Request timeout - Server scope (Global/Workspace/Agent) - Tool trust settings ### Remote (HTTP) Servers - HTTP headers (authentication tokens) - Request timeout - Server scope - Tool trust settings **Custom values override registry defaults**, allowing personal credentials and configuration. ## Examples ### Example 1: Add Registry Server ``` /mcp add ``` **Output**: ``` Select MCP server from registry: git-server (Git operations) github-server (GitHub integration) aws-tools (AWS operations) ``` ### Example 2: Add to Specific Agent ```bash kiro-cli mcp add --name git-server --agent rust-dev ``` Adds git-server to rust-dev agent configuration. ### Example 3: Customize with Environment Variables ```json { "mcpServers": { "github": { "command": "mcp-server-github", "args": ["--stdio"], "env": { "GITHUB_TOKEN": "$GITHUB_TOKEN" } } } } ``` Your token overrides registry defaults. ## Troubleshooting ### Issue: "MCP functionality has been disabled by your administrator" **Symptom**: Cannot use MCP at all **Cause**: Organization disabled MCP entirely **Solution**: Contact administrator for MCP access ### Issue: "Failed to retrieve MCP settings" **Symptom**: Error fetching MCP configuration **Cause**: Network issue or server error **Solution**: Temporary issue - retry later or contact administrator ### Issue: Cannot Add Custom Server **Symptom**: Server not in list **Cause**: Registry mode only allows registry servers **Solution**: Request administrator add server to registry ### Issue: Server Not in Registry **Symptom**: Needed server not available **Cause**: Not added to organization's registry **Solution**: Contact administrator to request server addition ## Related Features - [kiro-cli mcp](../commands/mcp.md) - MCP management commands - [/mcp](../slash-commands/mcp.md) - View MCP status - [Agent Configuration](agent-configuration.md) - Configure MCP servers in agents ## Limitations - Registry mode only for Pro-tier with IAM Identity Center - Cannot add servers not in registry - Administrator controls available servers - Custom servers not allowed in registry mode ## Technical Details **Registry Source**: Configured by administrator at organization level **Scope Options**: - Global: `~/.kiro/settings/mcp.json` - Workspace: `.kiro/settings/mcp.json` - Agent-specific: In agent configuration **Customization**: Environment variables and HTTP headers can be customized even in registry mode **Fallback**: If registry unavailable, MCP functionality disabled **Documentation**: For administrators, see [MCP Governance Documentation](https://kiro.dev/docs/cli/mcp/security/)

--- Document 4 ---
--- doc_meta: validated: 2026-01-29 commit: 5db28275 status: validated testable_headless: true category: tool title: use_aws description: Make AWS CLI API calls with service, operation, and parameters keywords: [use_aws, aws, cli, api, cloud, readonly, auto-approve] related: [execute-bash] --- # use_aws Make AWS CLI API calls with service, operation, and parameters. ## Overview > **Note**: This tool is used by the AI assistant to fulfill your requests. You don't invoke it directly - simply ask questions naturally, and the assistant will use this tool to make AWS API calls as needed. The use_aws tool executes AWS CLI commands with specified service, operation, and parameters. Supports all AWS services and operations. Read-only operations are auto-approved by default using a comprehensive list of 7,069 known readonly operations from the AWS Service Authorization Reference. Requires AWS CLI installed and configured. ## Usage > **Technical Reference**: The JSON examples below show the internal tool format used by the AI assistant. Users should not copy or type these - they are provided for developers and agent configuration authors only. ### Basic Usage ```json { "service_name": "s3", "operation_name": "list-buckets", "region": "us-east-1", "label": "List S3 buckets" } ``` ### Common Use Cases #### Use Case 1: List S3 Buckets ```json { "service_name": "s3", "operation_name": "list-buckets", "region": "us-east-1", "label": "List all S3 buckets" } ``` **What this does**: Executes `aws s3 list-buckets --region us-east-1`. Auto-approved if autoAllowReadonly enabled. #### Use Case 2: Describe EC2 Instances ```json { "service_name": "ec2", "operation_name": "describe-instances", "region": "us-west-2", "label": "Get EC2 instances" } ``` **What this does**: Lists EC2 instances in us-west-2. #### Use Case 3: Get S3 Object ```json { "service_name": "s3api", "operation_name": "get-object", "parameters": { "--bucket": "my-bucket", "--key": "file.txt", "outfile": "downloaded.txt" }, "region": "us-east-1", "label": "Download S3 object" } ``` **What this does**: Downloads object from S3 bucket. #### Use Case 4: Copy S3 File (High-Level Command) ```json { "service_name": "s3", "operation_name": "cp", "positional_args": ["s3://my-bucket/file.csv", "./local/"], "region": "us-east-1", "label": "Copy file from S3" } ``` **What this does**: Executes `aws s3 cp s3://my-bucket/file.csv ./local/`. Use `positional_args` for S3 high-level commands that require source/destination paths. #### Use Case 5: Sync S3 Directory ```json { "service_name": "s3", "operation_name": "sync", "positional_args": ["./data/", "s3://my-bucket/backup/"], "parameters": { "--exclude": "*.tmp" }, "region": "us-east-1", "label": "Sync local directory to S3" } ``` **What this does**: Syncs local directory to S3, excluding .tmp files. #### Use Case 6: With AWS Profile ```json { "service_name": "lambda", "operation_name": "list-functions", "region": "eu-west-1", "profile_name": "production", "label": "List Lambda functions in production" } ``` **What this does**: Uses specific AWS profile from `~/.aws/credentials`. ## Configuration Configure service restrictions in agent's `toolsSettings`: ```json { "toolsSettings": { "use_aws": { "allowedServices": ["s3", "lambda", "ec2"], "deniedServices": ["iam", "organizations"], "autoAllowReadonly": true } } } ``` | Option | Type | Default | Description | |--------|------|---------|-------------| | `allowedServices` | array | `[]` | Services accessible without prompting | | `deniedServices` | array | `[]` | Services to block. Evaluated before allow rules | | `autoAllowReadonly` | boolean | `true` | Auto-approve read-only operations (7,069 known readonly operations) | ## Parameters | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |-----------|------|----------|-------------| | `service_name` | string | Yes | AWS service (e.g., s3, ec2, lambda). Must not start with `-` | | `operation_name` | string | Yes | Operation to perform (e.g., list-buckets, describe-instances) | | `positional_args` | array | No | Positional arguments for S3 high-level commands (cp, mv, sync, rm). Source/destination paths | | `parameters` | object | No | Operation parameters as key-value pairs | | `region` | string | Yes | AWS region (e.g., us-east-1, eu-west-1) | | `profile_name` | string | No | AWS profile from ~/.aws/credentials | | `label` | string | Yes | Human-readable description | ## Parameter Format Use JSON syntax for parameters: ```json { "parameters": { "--bucket": "my-bucket", "--key": "file.txt", "--query": "Contents[?Size>`100`]", "outfile": "output.txt" } } ``` **Flags**: Use empty string as value: ```json { "parameters": { "--dry-run": "" } } ``` ## Positional Arguments Use `positional_args` for S3 high-level commands that require source/destination paths: ```json { "service_name": "s3", "operation_name": "cp", "positional_args": ["s3://bucket/source.txt", "/local/dest.txt"], "region": "us-east-1", "label": "Copy from S3" } ``` **Supported commands**: `s3 cp`, `s3 mv`, `s3 sync`, `s3 rm` Positional arguments are passed directly without `--` prefix, before any parameters. ``` ## Read-Only Operations Auto-approved by default (`autoAllowReadonly: true`). Uses a comprehensive list of 7,069 known readonly operations from the official AWS Service Authorization Reference. **Common readonly operations include**: - `describe-*`, `get-*`, `list-*` operations - `batch-get-*`, `search-*` operations - S3 CLI commands: `ls`, `presign` **Examples**: `list-buckets`, `describe-instances`, `get-object`, `search-resources` **To disable auto-approval** (require confirmation for all operations): ```json { "toolsSettings": { "use_aws": { "autoAllowReadonly": false } } } ``` ## Examples ### Example 1: List Lambda Functions ```json { "service_name": "lambda", "operation_name": "list-functions", "region": "us-east-1", "label": "List Lambda functions" } ``` ### Example 2: Get S3 Object with Query ```json { "service_name": "s3api", "operation_name": "list-objects-v2", "parameters": { "--bucket": "my-bucket", "--prefix": "logs/", "--max-items": "10" }, "region": "us-east-1", "label": "List recent logs" } ``` ### Example 3: Describe VPC ```json { "service_name": "ec2", "operation_name": "describe-vpcs", "parameters": { "--filters": "Name=isDefault,Values=true" }, "region": "us-west-2", "label": "Get default VPC" } ``` ## Troubleshooting ### Issue: Command Not Found **Symptom**: "Unable to spawn command" error **Cause**: AWS CLI not installed **Solution**: Install AWS CLI: `pip install awscli` or download from AWS ### Issue: Credentials Not Found **Symptom**: AWS credential error **Cause**: AWS CLI not configured **Solution**: Run `aws configure` to set up credentials ### Issue: Invalid Service Name **Symptom**: "Invalid service_name" error **Cause**: Service name starts with `-` **Solution**: Use valid service name without leading dash ### Issue: Permission Denied **Symptom**: Tool prompts for approval **Cause**: Service not in allowedServices or operation not read-only **Solution**: Add service to allowedServices or enable autoAllowReadonly ### Issue: Output Truncated **Symptom**: "... truncated" in output **Cause**: Output exceeds size limit **Solution**: Use `--query` parameter to filter results or `--max-items` to limit ## Related Features - [execute_bash](execute-bash.md) - Alternative for AWS CLI commands - [Agent Configuration](../features/agent-configuration.md) - Configure tool permissions ## Limitations - Requires AWS CLI installed - Requires AWS credentials configured - Output limited to prevent context overflow - No streaming output - Service name cannot start with `-` (security) - Parameters must conform to AWS CLI specification ## Technical Details **Aliases**: `use_aws`, `aws` **Command Format**: `aws --region <region> [--profile <profile>] <service> <operation> <parameters>` **Environment**: Includes user agent metadata for CloudTrail tracking **Output**: Returns JSON with exit_status, stdout, stderr **Truncation**: stdout and stderr each limited to 1/3 of MAX_TOOL_RESPONSE_SIZE **Permissions**: Read-only operations auto-approved by default. Write operations prompt unless service in allowedServices. Set `autoAllowReadonly: false` to require confirmation for all operations.

Overview The `/help` command displays all available slash commands with their descriptions and usage syntax. ## Usage ``` /help ``` ## Examples ### Show available commands ``` /help ``` Output: ``` Available Commands: /agent Select or list available agents Usage: /agent [agent-name|create <name>|edit [name]|swap <name>] /chat Load a previous session or start a new one Usage: /chat [save [--force] <path>|load <path>|new [prompt]] /clear Clear conversation history Usage: /clear /code Code intelligence workspace management Usage: /code [status|init|logs|overview|summary] /compact Compact conversation history Usage: /compact /context Manage context files or show token usage Usage: /context [add [--force] <path>...|remove <path>...|clear] /feedback Submit feedback, request features, or report issues Usage: /feedback /guide Get help with Kiro CLI features from the guide agent Usage: /guide [question] /help Show available commands Usage: /help /hooks View configured hooks Usage: /hooks /knowledge Manage knowledge base Usage: /knowledge [show|add <name> <path>|remove <name|path>|update <path>|clear|cancel] /mcp Show configured MCP servers Usage: /mcp /model Select or list available models Usage: /model [model-name] /paste Paste image from clipboard Usage: /paste /plan Switch to Plan agent for breaking down ideas into implementation plans Usage: /plan [prompt] /prompts Select or list available prompts Usage: /prompts [prompt-name] /quit Quit the application Usage: /quit /reply Open editor pre-filled with the last assistant message to compose a reply Usage: /reply /tools Show available tools Usage: /tools [trust-all|trust <name>|untrust <name>|reset] /usage Show billing and usage information Usage: /usage ``` ## TUI-Only Commands These commands are available in the TUI interface and appear in autocomplete, but are not listed in the `/help` panel: ``` /copy Copy last response to clipboard (use /transcript for full conversation) /editor Open $EDITOR to compose a prompt /exit Quit the application (alias for /quit) /spawn Spawn a new agent session with a task /theme Select a theme that looks best for your terminal /transcript Open conversation transcript in $PAGER (quit with q) ``` ## Use `/prompts` to see available prompts. ## Related - [/guide](guide.md) — Get help from the guide agent - [/tools](tools.md) — Show available tools - [/model](model.md) — Switch models - [/agent](agent-swap.md) — Switch agents

--- doc_meta: validated: 2026-04-09 commit: 4ae084db status: validated testable_headless: false category: slash_command title: /usage description: Show account-level usage limits and subscription plan information keywords: [usage, billing, credits, plan, limits, subscription] related: [model, settings] --- # /usage Show account-level usage limits and subscription plan information. ## Overview Retrieves your account's usage limits from the API, including subscription plan details, usage breakdowns by resource type, and bonus credits with expiry information. ## Usage ``` /usage ``` ## Output Returns JSON data containing: - `planName` - Your subscription plan title - `overagesEnabled` - Whether overage charges are enabled - `isEnterprise` - Whether this is an enterprise-managed plan - `usageBreakdowns[]` - Array of usage by resource type: - `resourceType`, `displayName` - `used`, `limit`, `percentage` - `currentOverages`, `overageRate`, `overageCharges`, `currency` - `bonusCredits[]` - Array of bonus credits: - `name`, `used`, `total`, `daysUntilExpiry` ## Limitations - Enterprise users see "Your plan is managed by admin" message - Requires valid API authentication ## Technical Details Calls `get_usage_limits()` API to retrieve account-level subscription and usage data. ## Examples ### Example 1: View Usage ``` /usage ``` **Output**: ``` Plan: Q Developer Pro | 2 usage breakdowns ``` With JSON data: ```json { "planName": "Q Developer Pro", "overagesEnabled": false, "isEnterprise": false, "usageBreakdowns": [ { "resourceType": "AGENTIC_REQUESTS", "displayName": "Agentic requests", "used": 150.0, "limit": 1000.0, "percentage": 15, "currentOverages": 0.0, "overageRate": 0.0, "overageCharges": null, "currency": "USD" } ], "bonusCredits": [ { "name": "Welcome bonus", "used": 10.0, "total": 50.0, "daysUntilExpiry": 25 } ] } ``` ## Troubleshooting ### Issue: "Your plan is managed by admin" **Symptom**: Message says plan is managed by admin **Cause**: Enterprise account with centrally managed billing **Solution**: Contact your organization's admin for usage details ### Issue: Failed to retrieve usage information **Symptom**: Error message about retrieval failure **Cause**: API authentication or connectivity issue **Solution**: Check your login status with `/whoami` and re-authenticate if needed

--- doc_meta: title: todo_list description: Task list tool for tracking multi-step work with create, complete, add, remove, and list commands category: tool keywords: [todo_list, todo, task, task_list, plan, checklist, tracking] related: [switch-to-execution] validated: 2026-04-08 commit: 1a984cb0 status: validated testable_headless: true --- ## Overview > This tool is used by the AI assistant to fulfill your requests. You don't invoke it directly - simply ask questions naturally. The task list tool creates and tracks multi-step tasks. It is used automatically when the assistant receives a task that requires multiple steps. Tasks are created before execution begins and marked off as they are completed. ## Usage The tool uses a `command` field to select the operation. ### create Create a new task list, replacing any existing one. - `tasks` (array, required) — List of tasks, each with: - `task_description` (string, required) — The task description - `details` (string, optional) — Additional details - `task_list_description` (string, required) — Brief summary of the overall task list ### complete Mark tasks as completed and record context. - `completed_task_ids` (array, required) — IDs of completed tasks - `context_update` (string, required) — Important context about what was done - `modified_files` (array, optional) — Paths of files modified during the task ### add Add new tasks to an existing task list. - `new_tasks` (array, required) — Tasks to add (same format as `create`) - `new_description` (string, optional) — Updated task list description if the goal changed ### remove Remove tasks from the list. - `remove_task_ids` (array, required) — IDs of tasks to remove - `new_description` (string, optional) — Updated task list description if the goal changed ### list Show all current tasks and their status. No parameters required. ## Examples ### Create a task list ```json { "command": "create", "task_list_description": "Add user authentication to the API", "tasks": [ {"task_description": "Create user model and migration"}, {"task_description": "Implement login endpoint"}, {"task_description": "Add JWT token generation"}, {"task_description": "Write tests for auth flow"} ] } ``` ### Mark a task complete ```json { "command": "complete", "completed_task_ids": ["1"], "context_update": "Created User model with email, password_hash fields. Migration adds users table.", "modified_files": ["src/models/user.rs", "migrations/001_create_users.sql"] } ``` ### Add tasks mid-execution ```json { "command": "add", "new_tasks": [ {"task_description": "Add rate limiting to login endpoint"} ], "new_description": "Add user authentication with rate limiting to the API" } ``` ## Troubleshooting ### "No tasks were provided" The `tasks` or `new_tasks` array is empty. Provide at least one task. ### "No task description was provided" The `task_list_description` is empty or missing for the `create` command. ### Task ID not found The ID in `completed_task_ids` or `remove_task_ids` doesn't match any existing task. Use the `list` command to see current task IDs. ## Related - [switch-to-execution](switch-to-execution.md) — Signals transition from planning to execution

--- doc_meta: validated: 2025-12-22 commit: 57090ffe status: validated testable_headless: false category: feature title: MCP Registry description: Enterprise MCP server security allowing administrators to control which servers users can access keywords: [mcp, registry, security, enterprise, admin, pro] related: [cmd-mcp, slash-mcp, agent-configuration] --- # MCP Registry Enterprise MCP server security allowing administrators to control which servers users can access. ## Overview Pro-tier customers using IAM Identity Center can have MCP server access controlled through an MCP registry. When configured by administrators, users can only use MCP servers explicitly allowed in the registry. Provides centralized security for enterprise deployments. ## How It Works **Without Registry** (default): - Users can add any MCP server - Servers configured in agent files or via CLI - No central control **With Registry** (enterprise): - Administrator configures allowed servers - Users select from registry list - Cannot add custom servers - Centralized security and security ## Adding Servers (Registry Mode) ### In Chat ``` /mcp add ``` Shows interactive list of servers from organization's registry. ### In CLI ```bash # Add specific server kiro-cli mcp add --name myserver # Add to workspace kiro-cli mcp add --scope workspace # Add to specific agent kiro-cli mcp add --agent myagent # Interactive selection kiro-cli mcp add ``` **Note**: Server name must match registry. Cannot add custom servers. ## Removing Servers ### In Chat ``` /mcp remove ``` Interactive menu to select server to remove. ### In CLI ```bash kiro-cli mcp remove --name <server-name> ``` ## Viewing Available Servers ### In Chat ``` /mcp list ``` Shows: - All locally configured MCP servers - Server status and configuration - Available tools from each server ## Customization (Registry Mode) Even with registry, you can customize: ### Local (stdio) Servers - Environment variables (API keys, paths) - Request timeout - Server scope (Global/Workspace/Agent) - Tool trust settings ### Remote (HTTP) Servers - HTTP headers (authentication tokens) - Request timeout - Server scope - Tool trust settings **Custom values override registry defaults**, allowing personal credentials and configuration. ## Examples ### Example 1: Add Registry Server ``` /mcp add ``` **Output**: ``` Select MCP server from registry: git-server (Git operations) github-server (GitHub integration) aws-tools (AWS operations) ``` ### Example 2: Add to Specific Agent ```bash kiro-cli mcp add --name git-server --agent rust-dev ``` Adds git-server to rust-dev agent configuration. ### Example 3: Customize with Environment Variables ```json { "mcpServers": { "github": { "command": "mcp-server-github", "args": ["--stdio"], "env": { "GITHUB_TOKEN": "$GITHUB_TOKEN" } } } } ``` Your token overrides registry defaults. ## Troubleshooting ### Issue: "MCP functionality has been disabled by your administrator" **Symptom**: Cannot use MCP at all **Cause**: Organization disabled MCP entirely **Solution**: Contact administrator for MCP access ### Issue: "Failed to retrieve MCP settings" **Symptom**: Error fetching MCP configuration **Cause**: Network issue or server error **Solution**: Temporary issue - retry later or contact administrator ### Issue: Cannot Add Custom Server **Symptom**: Server not in list **Cause**: Registry mode only allows registry servers **Solution**: Request administrator add server to registry ### Issue: Server Not in Registry **Symptom**: Needed server not available **Cause**: Not added to organization's registry **Solution**: Contact administrator to request server addition ## Related Features - [kiro-cli mcp](../commands/mcp.md) - MCP management commands - [/mcp](../slash-commands/mcp.md) - View MCP status - [Agent Configuration](agent-configuration.md) - Configure MCP servers in agents ## Limitations - Registry mode only for Pro-tier with IAM Identity Center - Cannot add servers not in registry - Administrator controls available servers - Custom servers not allowed in registry mode ## Technical Details **Registry Source**: Configured by administrator at organization level **Scope Options**: - Global: `~/.kiro/settings/mcp.json` - Workspace: `.kiro/settings/mcp.json` - Agent-specific: In agent configuration **Customization**: Environment variables and HTTP headers can be customized even in registry mode **Fallback**: If registry unavailable, MCP functionality disabled **Documentation**: For administrators, see [MCP Governance Documentation](https://kiro.dev/docs/cli/mcp/security/)
